result:
I want to talk about how beautiful it is that we're all just temporary arrangements of stardust arguing on the internet.

Right now, as I'm typing this, there's a whale singing somewhere in the Pacific at a frequency that literally no other creature on Earth can hear the way it does. There's a little girl in Kyoto who's just discovered that if you spin fast enough in a kimono, the sleeves make a sound like wings. There's an old man in Patagonia who's laughing because he finally remembered the name of the dog he had when he was seven. All of this is happening at the same time as someone is stress-eating cereal at 3am while crying over a text message.

The scale of it all is completely insane.

I keep thinking about how every person you pass on the street is carrying an entire universe around in their head. Their own private collection of memories, inside jokes, irrational fears, favorite songs, and that one specific way their grandmother used to pronounce "spaghetti." Most of those universes will never overlap with yours. You'll never know that the person who just held the door for you is secretly obsessed with 19th century lighthouse architecture or that the barista who made your coffee once almost became a professional juggler.

We're all these incredibly intricate, ridiculous, glorious accidents. Consciousness somehow emerged from chemistry and now here we are, stress-sweating over whether our group chat is mad at us while simultaneously being able to look up at the same sky that looked down on Cleopatra and Genghis Khan and some teenager in 4000 BCE who was probably also worrying about whether their crush liked them back.

It's all so stupid and perfect.

Sometimes I lie on the floor of my apartment and just feel the weight of my own body pressing into the ground. I think about how the floor is pressing back with exactly the same force (thanks, Newton), and how that force is ultimately electromagnetic repulsion between atoms. I'm being held up by invisible force fields generated by ancient stardust that learned how to cry at Pixar movies.

I don't know. The universe spent 13.8 billion years setting up this exact stupid moment where I could ramble at you about whales and cereal and existential floor time. That's kind of amazing when you think about it.

What a time to be a conscious arrangement of atoms.

What's something that's been making you feel wonder or absurdity lately?
